Step 5: 	The Grand Offer, Part 2

Jon: 	We’re back with part 2 of the grand offer, and this is the price. We
covered the product in the first video, the second, here, is the price.
As you can see with the mind map on your screen, we have a faux
price, which we’ll get into, the bonuses, we’re going to restate the faux
price, the cost of loss, that’s if you don’t take action. Then, you’re
going to have to give a reason for the discount, and we’re going to go
into the whole thing that I call the cause. I think you’ll get a lot out of
that segment.
The price drop, and then the price reveal, in other words, we’re going
to do the price drop, then price reveal, and then I’m going to teach you
the 3X triple guarantee, which is really cool. Alright let’s get started.
We begin by introducing what’s called a faux price. This is a price
that’s roughly twice that of your actual price. It’s a fake price. And this
is done to anchor your viewer, your prospect, to a higher price, before
adding in all your bonuses.
Notice that there’s no bonuses that have been added into the grand
offer yet. So, after you do that, your actual price will be so much more
appealing when you get to the offer. So we’re going to tell the viewers
that the value of your product, that value of your product, is X.
And that X is going to be about twice what your sale price is. Here’s
how this is going to work. I’ll give you some example slides.
I simply say, “The price of the Every Other Day Diet system, sold by
itself, is valued at $97.” I’ve actually sold it for $97, so I could literally
say $97, but I don’t. I say, “It’s valued at $97,” because that’s the initial
value of it, because it comes with all this other stuff. Right?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 13 --

2
So, I anchor $97 in the prospect’s mind. Now, at this point, you are
probably thinking what I was thinking when I first discovered this.
There’s a few techniques that I discovered by looking at everybody
else’s winning video sales letters. This is one of them. This works,
and I’m not really sure why it works, but it does work.
My immediate reaction, when I first saw this, was like, “Oh, wow, $97,
that’s too expensive, I’m going to window out,” but that’s not what
happens. That’s not what happens.
I think the reason why this works is perceived value. Because you’ve
just built up your product, or service, to have an extraordinary value.
So, when you deliver a price, even if the price is high, like if you got a
$3,000 product, and you built up the value, they could be thinking it’s
$6,000 or $10,000.
So, if you say it’s valued at $6,000, then they might be, “Oh, boy.
That’s pretty expensive, but I’ll keep watching.” And this is what
happens. So I say, “It’s valued at $97, and the next part is this is
where you want to bring that value up, you’re going to bring in your
bonuses.
You’re going to add specific price values to each of these bonuses as
well, so you’re going to add a value that they would be sold at. If you
don’t know what I mean by would be sold at value stuff, go back and
look at the first video.
Graphics are important here, because the better your bonuses will
look, the hotter your product will appear to be. So, if your bonuses are
outstanding, assuming that you have bonuses – and, by the way, if you
don’t have bonuses, you should really consider putting bonuses in
there – it doesn’t matter what it is. If it’s a service, you might say, “You
get this discount or this for free,” “we’ll do 2 for one here.”
You can do any kind of bonuses. They don’t have to be physical
bonuses. So, obviously, the look isn’t nearly as important for that kind
of bonus. But if you have physical bonuses, the look is hot. And let
me tell you, the psychology here is pretty strong. If the bonuses look
really appealing, the product’s going to appear to be hotter.
And here’s some examples. I begin by saying, “Yet, you’re not just
receiving the Every Other Day Diet system today,” Do you need for
me to explain what I’ve just done here? First of all, I begin with the
word “yet.” “You’re not just receiving,” my name of my product,
“today.” I’m just assuming that they’re receiving the product today.
Notice that is continual throughout the entire video sales letter. The
close part, especially, the grand offer. So, “You’re not just,” and I
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 13 --

3
highlighted the word today. “Just for a watching this presentation, I’d
like to give you, free of charge, these value-add bonuses, to help you
lose weight even faster.”
Now, this is very critical what I’ve done here, “I’m going to give you,
free of charge, a value-add bonus,” I call it value-add, some people call
it plug-ins, whatever. You can call it whatever you want, I like value-
add bonuses.
And I always say, and this is the big reason why I think a lot of video
sales letters crash and burn here, is I will see people go, “I’m going to
also give you these free bonuses,” and then they just go into the
bonuses, but they don’t tell the user what the bonuses are for. They
don’t give a benefit. All they do is talk about how great the bonuses
are, or they assume that the word bonus is enough.
No, I’m giving them these value-add bonuses. Why am I giving it to
them? “Because I want you to lose weight even faster.”
Now, I could say anything here. In fact, some of these bonuses, it
would be even better to say, “Lose weight even faster and look better
faster,” because the very first one is all about looking better faster. But
you get the idea, “Lose weight even faster.”
This is where, again, I will read through, it’s not a true paraphrase,
because I’ve got this. This is how my bonuses actually look, and I call
it the free gift.
It’s attractive-looking, and I’ve got the right graphic design elements
that make it look like it’s a really meaty thing.
So, I’ll read through the copy by saying, “Okay, the first gift that you’re
going to get today is perfect posture in 30 days. And a lot of people
don’t know this, but you can become almost instantly sexier and more
attractive by improving your posture. Posture has been shown to be
one of the most sexy things that men and women look at, is really
strong posture.”
Notice, my wording isn’t exactly perfect, and that’s okay. You don’t
have to be perfect. “And I’m going to give you this 23-page digital
ebook. It’s normally valued at $37,” and I say valued at $37. “I’m
going to give it to you free today, when you order. And not only that,
but you’re also going to get, Cholesterol Lies. This book’s going to
save you money, as well as possibly save your life, because there’s so
many lies about cholesterol out there. And Dr. Duane Graveline, MD,
was one of the lunar mission surgeons for NASA. The guy knows what
he’s talking about when it comes down to being an everything doctor.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

4
“And, let me tell you, the truth about cholesterol-lowering drugs will
shock you, and you need to know this, because it can save you a
fortune in medical bills, and it can possibly save your life. It’s $27; I’m
giving it to you for free.”
“Finally, you’re going to get one of the most important bonuses that I
could possibly ever give you, and that’s, Hormones 101. It’s what you
really need to know about your hormone health. And the reason why
that’s so important – it’s not just for women, it’s for men too – is
because especially past the age of 30, your hormones begin to
change. And once they begin to change, weight loss can become next
to impossible. But once you get revitalization of the hormones, once
you get them back to your youthful state, weight loss almost takes care
of itself. So, this is going to really help you lose weight faster. It’s $57;
I’m giving it to you for free.”
So, I’ve built a price, I’ve built a case, I’ve just ad libbed this completely
off the top of my head.
I may, if I stumbled too much, I may stop and then I’ll pause for 5
seconds, and then begin recording again, and record it again and edit
it out, of course. You’ll see those tutorials in the Completing Your VSL
segment.
But, that’s basically how I do it. Now, you want to move to restating
the faux price, but this time we’re going to add the values in. And the
key phrase here is “if sold separately,” or “if I was to sell you these
bonuses.”
This is very important for the FTC. It’s very important to keep the ethic
of the offer intact, too.
Then, you’re going to hint at a better deal. Here’s some example
slides. Notice the 2 words. What’s the first 2 words that you see?
“Now, see.”
So, that’s kind of a cool thing to do. “Now, you can see why the Every
Other Day Diet system is valued at $97. Even without the bonuses, it’s
a steal.”
“And if you bought the bonuses by themselves, your total investment
today would be over $200.” Notice I say, “And if you bought the
bonuses by themselves.” So, if you where to buy them, I’m not saying
that I sell them, if you were to buy them. “However, just for watching
this presentation today, I have a much better deal coming your way.
Just keep watching, because it won’t last much longer.”
Look what I’ve done here, I’ve positioned this as a better deal, just for
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 13 --

5
watching the presentation.
So they go, “Oh, okay, so is that better than $200 or is it better than
$97? I’m not really sure, so I’m going to keep watching,” I’ve built
curiosity now, around the price. That’s the reason why the faux price
probably works, is because once you establish a faux price, and then
you add to that with the bonuses, now they’re not sure how much this
thing costs, and they’re kind of curious, at this point. They want to see.
But I do something else. What do I do? Just keep watching.
Remember leading? You’re always leading. Well, you haven’t been
leading yet through this whole offer, grand offer, because the grand
offer leads itself. But now, when we start talking about a deal coming,
we want to open a loop and lead further.
“Better deal, keep watching, deal isn’t going to last much longer, it isn’t
going to last forever.” So if you say, “It won’t last forever,” that would
be a great way to say that. In my case, it really won’t last much longer.
So now, it’s time to describe the financial loss of not taking immediate
action.
Notice how upbeat, positive, powerful we’ve been, all the benefits, all
this, the benefits, the benefits, the benefits? And now, what do we do?
We change the modality. We change the modality from pleasure to
pain.
And we’re going to describe the financial loss of them not taking
immediate action. Here’s some example slides for you.
I begin with “Of course,” because that’s such a great way to segment
into this, I think. “Of course, you could chose,” giving people the power
to chose.
“You could chose to continue on the path you’re on, right now.” I’ve
emphasized “right now,” because, subconsciously, you’re going to pick
up “right now,” you’re not going to pick up the entire sentence. “And
pay 10, 20, maybe even 100 times this much money in the near future,
in the form of more expensive food.” And then, a little side note here.
“Yeah, my system will probably save you money on food.” And “save
you money” is highlighted, obviously.
“Or, those new clothes. And I’m not talking about the fun new clothes,
no. These are the ones that you’re going to be forced to buy, as your
hips, thighs, and waist continue to get bigger and bigger?”
Do you notice how I ended that sentence in a question, even though
it’s not truly a question? There’s a reason for that, covered in the vocal
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 13 --

6
tonality technique video. It’s kind of cool.
So, what have I emphasized here? The word “fun.” Subliminal
highlight the word “fun.” But I’m also talking about the expenses that’s
going to happen if they don’t take action.
So, I’ve covered, right now, the food, the clothes, but not the fun
clothes, because if I just said, “Oh, you’ll have to buy new clothes,
somebody may come back and say, “Well, I’ll have to buy new clothes
when I lose weight anyway.” So, I made this, “Oh, not the fun new
clothes, you’re going to have to buy the fat new clothes, which is a real
pain.” I’m emphasizing pain.
And finally. I conclude. I like to give 3 painful elements here, the cost
of loss, “And let’s not even talk about the medical bills, medication
costs, time off work. This is all going to eventually happen to you, if
you keep doing what you’re doing right now.”
So, I’ve laid it pretty horrible picture down. “If you keep doing what
you’re doing right now, these things will happen.”
If you have a service-oriented business, you need to describe, “If you
do not take advantage of our service, here’s the pain, the
inconveniences that your life will experience.
If you do a party service, if you work with hotels, it’s all about pain of
disservice. In other words, what’s going to happen when people don’t
have this done for them? They’re going to have to figure it out, they’re
going to have to spend all their time and energy doing that.
And what’s your time worth to you? But in this case, it’s very easy
when it’s a product, because you can just say what the pain is of not
taking action, they know they need to take action, what is the pain of
it?
So, I’ve made the pain very tangible, in the form of food, clothes, and
medical bills. So, that’s what you want to look for, the most tangible
elements you can create, to make real live, totally honest, realistic
pain, if someone doesn’t make a decision.
And I conclude with the slide that we’ve already talked about, “And that
isn’t what I want for you.” Remember that slide? “It’s not what I want
for you, you’ve now reestablished rapport, again, and again. You’ve
got rapport going through the whole VSL. But now, you’ve got the
friendly rapport, “That’s not what I want for you.
“So, let’s make this a really easy decision, okay?” What have I done
here? I’m exiting out of the cost of loss by making something very
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 13 --

7
positive, “I’m going to make this an easy decision.” What have I done
here? Well, first of all, I’ve entered into a slight agreement with the
prospect. When you say, “Got it! Deal! Okay?” those are all slight
agreements.
You get somebody going, “Okay,” that’s what you want to say. If I go,
“Deal?” they go, “Deal” in their mind. “Okay?” “Okay.” If I get
someone saying, “Okay, yes, deal,” what’s going to happen toward the
pitch? More chance of them buying.
“So, let’s make this a really easy decision,” that is a key, key phrase.
Because if you make this an easy decision, if you are telling them this
is an easy decision, then if they don’t make that decision to buy, how
does that make them feel?
“Well, it’s easy, and I’m not doing it.” It creates a little bit of internal
conflict.
Okay, now that your prospect has been set up to receive that better
deal, you’ve got to give him or her a valid reason for that reduction in
price.
This is probably the biggest mistake I see in video sales letters. I have
made it in the past myself. I was consulting with one of the biggest
gurus. Basically, I call him a wordsmithing guru, and we were going
through my most recent video sales letters. I think he makes like
$20,000 a day to consult with people, an insane amount of money.
But he’s really a savvy dude. He was just all onboard with everything
until – this was one of my older video sales letters – I got to the point
where I did not give a valid reason, and he’s like, “Hey, hey, wait, wait,
wait! Why? Why are you giving me this better deal?” I go, “Uh, I don’t
know.”
And it was at that point I decided he’s absolutely right. I need to give
people a very valid reason.”
What I did is I carried it a little further than that. I created the
establishment of a cause, I’m not saying I created this; in other words,
it didn’t exist before me. I just started creating it in my video sales
letters.
The best possible thing you can do, as a valid reason for lowering the
price, is to establish a cause, something that people want to be a part
of that’s larger than themselves. So, ipso facto, you need to increase
your reach.
If you charge $300 for this, you can’t have as many people as part of
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 13 --

8
the cause. And the cause is more important, so now you need to lower
the price.
And all this is true, by the way. I did establish a cause. And by
establishing a cause, it just means that you just create something
that’s bigger than everybody.
If you cannot do this, by the way, it’s not an absolute necessity. I don’t
want you to think, “Oh, gosh, I can’t create a cause over dry cleaning,”
or whatever business you may be in. Some services can’t pull this off.
You can’t have a larger calling to brick-and-mortar businesses,
sometimes.
But in a lot of cases, you can. And if you can, that’s great. If you
cannot, you just want to simply say, “Look, I’m offering this as an
online only special,” and that’s all you need to do. “I’m going to give
you a discount as an online only special.” All that does is assume that
there’s an offline price. And, if at all possible, create an offline product
with the real price to it. That’s the best, most ethical thing you can do.
I’ve done that many times.
A cause is powerful, and let me give you an example. This is right out
of my real cause. “I feel you deserve to know why I’m about to offer
you such a massive discount today.” That slides going to grab
anyone’s attention. “You need to know why, I’m about to offer you
such a massive discount.”
So, what have I told them? There’s a massive discount coming, but
you need to know why. Makes curiosity, right? Builds that curiosity.
“So, let me share my dream with you. It’s a cause that I’m inviting you
to join, along with thousands of other folks just like you, who are
already in this thing. They’re already doing it.” I’m paraphrasing.
“And people like you, who are ready to reinvent their body, reinvigorate
their energy, and replace ugly health-draining blubber with life-
producing, calorie-eating, sexy muscle tone.” Notice what I’m doing
here. I’m going wordsmithing nuts, because I’m building into the
cause. And once I get to the cause, I’ve totally got their attention, at
this point.
This is a great place to do the wordsmithing. Because when you say,
“I want to tell you why this is going to be so much cheaper,” no one’s
going to exit there. If they’ve exited, they’ve exited a long time ago, or
they’re not going to exit at all, that they’re not going to exit there.
So, take advantage of that point. That is called a sticky point. All the
sticky points, by the way, I haven’t pointed them out all along, but this
is a sticky point. This is why I am hammering home the benefits, once
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 13 --

9
again. So, let’s go back and look at that.
I’m going to share a dream with them, a cause, and, “It’s for people just
like you, who are ready to reinvent their body, reinvigorate their
energy, and replace ugly health-draining blubber with life-producing,
calorie-eating, sexy muscle.”
So, I’ve really, really hyped this pretty heavily here, and this is a good
place to do it. And I include one more element. “And above all,
share…” you could say, “your new body, your new life and body with
the world,” rather than “that new body life and body with the world.”
Either one works. I’m talking about a group of people, so I use the
word “that.”
But using “you” works. It doesn’t matter that it’s grammatically not
correct.
So, “Above all, share.” That’s the key, “Share your body with the
world.” And I tell them about the cause. “A few years ago, I set an
inspiring goal. I decided to dedicate myself to helping at least 1-million
men and women lose at least 10 pounds, and keep those 10 pounds
off for life. Since then, I’ve helped thousands and thousands lose far
more than just 10 pounds.”
That’s how I would word that, by the way. “Oh, yeah, it’s far more than
just 10 pounds. Yet, I thought 10 pounds was a great start,
because…” What have I done here? Anchor drop. “Why do I feel like
10 pounds is a great start? That’s 10-million pounds in total. Can you
imagine it?” I asked someone to imagine. That’s a dream state.
“How much joy would that create in the world around you? How many
happier kids would that make, knowing that you would have less
obesity among children?” This is where I paraphrase, “…less obesity
among children, their parents have so much more energy to give their
kids, to play with their kids. 10-million pounds! How many other
people will be so inspired by this cause? 10-million pounds!” I just
keep emphasizing the cause.
“The bottom line, I decide that I absolutely need to let as many folks as
possible join in on this cause.”
“So, here’s the deal. I need,” notice it says need? Need is, of course,
subliminally highlighted, because need is good. “You need to join this
cause. And it’s a cause I call, 10-million pounds down. The good
news? That means a much lower price for you, because I want you to
join the cause. And I don’t want finances to stop anyone from joining
this cause. So, let’s add your 10 pounds, or 25, or even 100 pounds
down to the cause, and let’s do it starting right now.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 13 --

10
So, I’m slightly paraphrasing that slide, but this is how I read video
sales letters.
So, add, “starting right now.” That’s a pretty powerful subliminal, add
to cart, because that’s going to be coming up, add to cart, starting right
now.
But, look what I’ve done. I have built a massive inclusion here. I have
built a group – and there is a group, by the way, 10-million pounds
down – and out of those, I want 10-million pounds down, 1-million
people losing 10 pounds or more. I’m asking them to add their 10
pounds, or 25, or 100, to the cause. In other words, I want you to join
something bigger than your own weight lose goals. And “That’s why
I’m offering it to you at such a value, because I want as many people in
here as possible.” Make sense? And then I say, “Starting right now.”
Now, here, we get into the actual sale price; because, at this point,
somebody is completely ready to see this. They’re so curious, they’re
ready to buy, they understand the cause, and we’re going to get there
by means of a price drop. This means we start with the highest price
that we’ve mentioned in the video so far. In my example, this was over
$200. I think it was $217. And then, we stepped down 3 times, to get
to the presentation discount price. Or, in my case, the cause discount
price.
So, here’s some example slides, very simple to do. “That means, that
today, you will not be paying the retail value of the Every Other Day
Diet and the bonuses. Not even close. Your investment today will not
be $217, as it should be, but it’s not going to be today. It’s not even
going to be half that. It’s not even going to be $67.”
“Your total investment today,” notice on this slide I show the entire
package. “Your total investment today,” and I use the word
investment, I don’t say “your total price,” because they are investing in
their body, they’re investing in their future, they’re investing in their
lives.
So, “Your total investment today, which includes the Every Other Day
Diet system, and all the bonuses you see above, is now,” I emphasized
now as an anchor, “only one payment of just $47.”
Now, that’s a very powerful thing that I just want to go back over and
show you what I’ve done. “Your total investment,” notice that the
words “investment” and “now” are highlighted in black. And “all,” I just
highlighted that in red, because I wanted them to make sure they get
everything they see above.
“Is now,” talk about a massive anchor drop, “now, only one payment.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 13 --

11
So I’m telling the subconscious mind, “Now, make one payment.”
Notice the “one payment.” This is a trick that very few marketers use.
They’ll just say, “Is now only $47.” But you know what? People have
been geared to think in terms of payments from watching QVC, from
watching all these price things, all these Amazon buys. There’s all
sorts of, like payout patterns and stuff like that, where you do recurring
payments and junk like that. And you see this on Home Shopping
Network and all that kind of stuff. I said Amazon; I meant Home
Shopping Network.
So, I say, “Only one payment of just $47.” That breaks any sort of,
“Oh, wow. It’s going to cost me $47. That’s like ridiculously cheap.”
And that’s what you want people to think.
So, you’ve walked them down from $217, in my case, to $47. Your
case may be from $3,000 to $1,500, $3,000 to $1,000, who knows?
At this point, you can choose to include your buy button reveal, the
magic buy button. It’s a very good place, by the way. It’s my favorite
place. This is the place where I’ll say, “Look below this video right
now,” and boom, the button pops in. I think it’s the best place.
And, again, this is the magic buy button or related technology. I have
videos on both. This is where you want to reveal it, underneath the
video. And you can put graphics and testimonials. You could even put
a full sales page copy underneath the video, at this point. And that’s
actually recommended, because you’re going to go into a different
modality. And, don’t worry, the scripts to do that have been given to
you. You can just download them in the downloads area.
I want to give you an example video, in case you don’t know what a
magic buy button is. It’s something I created about 2½ years ago, with
my programmer, and it’s been probably the most ripped off code on the
internet. But in case you haven’t seen it, this is what it would look like.
“So, our off-the-shelf price is usually $27. Yet not for you, if you take
this see it, one-time-only offer now. Click the add to cart button below
this video now, and you’ll see the EODD.”
Okay. Did you notice how the button just appeared? It appeared right
when I told it to. That’s just in a line of Java script code, and we’ve
given that to you in the magic buy button download. So, check that
out, along with some tutorials on how to use it.
Now comes the guarantee. Okay, you must create a guarantee.
Hopefully, you have one for your product. Solid guarantees increase
conversions by 50%, hands down.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 13 --

12
Now, the most effective guarantee that I’ve ever used, aside from
offering double your money back, actually, a friend of mine offered
triple their money back – which gave me the idea for the triple
guarantee, by the way. He offered triple their money back.
Now, if you have an awesome product and you know that you got low
refunds and you can afford those refunds, then knock yourself out.
Because that’s going to be the most, by far and away, effective.
But the next to best that costs you no extra money is the 3X triple
guarantee.
The benefit to the triple guarantee is that you don’t risk paying anything
back, other than the cost of the product. And again, like I said, you
might want to consider that if your refunds are really low.
And it’s a good idea to show your graphics at this point, including any
kind of site security graphics you have, like the BBB or Trust Guard or
guarantee seals. You definitely want a guarantee seal. Any
authoritative graphics, at this point, if you have them, anything like that
works really, really well here.
So, here are some example slides to show you how this would look.
By the way, this is the best way to position your purchase, when it
comes to a guarantee.
“Don’t decide right now.” Now, you may be thinking, “Whoa, whoa,
whoa, whoa. You just completely went backwards. You’ve told people
that, ‘Right now, you’re going to get this; right now, you’ve already
done this; right now, you’ve already done this.’”
The reason I’m doing this is: 1) it’s a psychological pattern break, and
2) it leads into the guarantee.
So, in essence, they don’t have to decide right now. They can decide
in 60 days. That’s the trick here. And it’s really powerful for sales
close.
So, I show everything on the screen. I show my security verified, my
McAfee. In reality, I would be showing like VeriSign and whatever, all
the stuff that I have. And I show “100% satisfaction guaranteed.”
So, I say, “Listen, don’t decide right now. Take advantage of my
unconditional triple guarantee. Just try the Every Other Day Diet
system for yourself for a full 60 days.” That’s my length of guarantee.
Yours may be 30, yours may be a year.
“If you do not drop all the weight you expect.” Notice I don’t give a
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 13 --

13
number here, by the way. And if you’re in a financial market, you
should not say, “If you don’t make $10-million.” You can say, “If you do
not make all the money that you expect to make in those 60 short
days,” if it’s 60 short days.
So, that’s level one of my triple guarantee. “If you do not drop all the
weight you expect in those 60 short days, or if you do not lose at least
10% of your unwanted body weight in the first 30 days.” That’s the
second level. Notice I changed from 60 days to 30 days.
So, I say, “If you don’t lose the weight you expect in 60, if you don’t
lose 10% of your body weight in 30, or,” and this is where I get to the
ridiculous part, if you’re looking at the mind map, “even if you just don’t
like the way my books are designed, the font face I used, you name it,
it doesn’t matter, I will gladly refund every penny, no questions, no
hassles.”
That’s the formula for the 3X guarantee and triple guarantee, and it
works so well. It works so well, because it 2 legitimate angles that you
will give somebody their money back at 60 days. “If you don’t see the
results you want in 60 days, if you don’t see a specific results in 30
days, and even if you don’t like the way I talk, the font of the book.”
That’s just an absurd thing. That basically just says, “Look, I am so
confident in this product that I’m going to make this ridiculous
guarantee.” So, that’s what goes through the mind of the viewer.
It’s triple guarantee. So no questions, no hassles. That’s how I’ll
always end.
Hopefully, this was very clear in that you now know exactly how to
frame the price part of your video sales letter. And getting into the
grand offer, the price is huge. We’ve anchored the price. We’ve gone
through the faux price. We’ve gone through the price reduction. Now
your prospect is ready to buy.
It’s essential, however, that you go into the next part of the grand offer.
